title: Brand vs Budget
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you resist brand pressure while making smart purchases?
What strategies help you evaluate value vs. brand appeal?

options.label: Buying brands to impress
options.reflection: Buying brands just to impress others often leads to unnecessary expenses that strain your budget and don't provide lasting value.

options.label: Buying within budget and need
options.reflection: Perfect! Sticking to your budget and actual needs ensures your money is spent on things that provide real value to your life.

options.label: Always getting latest models
options.reflection: Purchasing the latest models often means paying premium prices for marginal improvements, which can significantly impact your finances.

options.label: Following trendy purchases
options.reflection: Trendy purchases often lose value quickly and may not serve your actual needs, making them poor financial decisions.

stages.prompt: Why do branded items often cost more?

options.label: Significantly better materials and construction
options.reflection: Often the quality difference doesn't justify the price premium. Many non-branded items offer similar quality at lower prices.

options.label: They use rare and expensive materials
options.reflection: Branded items don't necessarily use rare materials. The premium is often for the brand name rather than material rarity.

options.label: Higher marketing and brand premiums
options.reflection: Exactly! A significant portion of the cost of branded items goes toward marketing and brand maintenance rather than product quality.

options.label: They are all handmade by skilled artisans
options.reflection: Most branded items are mass-produced, not handmade. The premium comes from branding, not craftsmanship.

stages.prompt: What's the best approach to purchasing decisions?

options.label: Choose based on brand reputation alone
options.reflection: Choosing based on brand alone often leads to overpaying for items that may not meet your specific needs or provide good value.

options.label: Go with what's popular among peers
options.reflection: Following popularity trends can lead to unnecessary purchases that don't align with your actual needs or financial situation.

options.label: Pick based on appearance and status
options.reflection: Choosing items based on appearance and status often leads to purchases that look good but don't provide practical value.

options.label: Focus on value and actual need
options.reflection: Perfect! Evaluating value and need before purchasing helps ensure your money is spent wisely on items that truly benefit you.

stages.prompt: How does brand pressure affect your finances?

options.label: Brand pressure causes unnecessary expense
options.reflection: Exactly! The pressure to buy certain brands often leads to overspending on items that don't provide proportionally greater value.

options.label: It helps increase your savings rate
options.reflection: Brand pressure typically decreases savings as it encourages spending more on premium products rather than saving.

options.label: Branded items are always good investments
options.reflection: Most consumer goods depreciate over time, branded or not. Few branded items hold or increase their value.

options.label: It guarantees better quality purchases
options.reflection: Brand does not guarantee quality. Many non-branded items offer equivalent or better quality at lower prices.

stages.prompt: What should you consider before buying a branded item?

options.label: How much status it will give you
options.reflection: Focusing on status often leads to purchases that strain your finances without providing practical value to your life.

options.label: Whether you actually need the item and can afford it
options.reflection: Perfect! Assessing actual need and affordability should always come before considering any branded purchase.

options.label: How much it will impress others
options.reflection: Making purchases to impress others often leads to financial strain for temporary social gains.

options.label: Whether it's currently trending
options.reflection: Trending items may not serve your actual needs and often lose their appeal quickly, making them poor financial choices.
